article_title: Elixir 1.20 Ships as a Gradually Typed Language, Capping a Type-System Effort Begun in 2022

content_preview.title: Elixir 1.20 Ships as a Gradually Typed Language, Capping a Type-System Effort Begun in 2022
content_preview.summary: The June 3 stable release completes Elixir's first type-system milestone: the compiler now infers and gradually type-checks every program for verified bugs without requiring any annotations.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Elixir core team has shipped Elixir 1.20.0, the stable release that the project frames as the moment the language becomes, in its own words, "now a gradually typed language." According to the [official release announcement](https://elixir-lang.org/blog/2026/06/03/elixir-v1-20-0-released/), the version landed on June 3, 2026; the [changelog](https://hexdocs.pm/elixir/changelog.html) records it as `v1.20.0 (2026-06-03)` and the [GitHub release tag](https://github.com/elixir-lang/e...

editor_notes.content_quality: Polished, technically precise News piece (737 words) on the Elixir 1.20 GA, framed as a legitimate follow-up that centers on what's new (the June 3 stable release) and cross-references the prior RC.4 coverage rather than re-narrating it.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (official release announcement) confirms the 'now a gradually typed language', 'perform type inference and gradually type check every Elixir program, without introducing type annotations', 'typing violations that are guaranteed to fail at runtime if executed', 'In 2022, we announced the effort…', the sound-typing definition, the build-tool benchmark line, the June 03 2026 date, and the José Valim author credit. source-1 (changelog) confirms 'v1.20.0 (2026-06-03)', the 'Erlang/OTP 27+ … compatible with Erlang/OTP 29' line, all seven new stdlib helpers (Integer.popcount/1, Integer.ceil_div/2, List.first!/1, List.last!/1, IO.iodata_empty?/1, Process.get_label/1, Regex.import/1), and the compilation-time note. source-2 (GitHub release tag) confirms the four section headings, Erlang/OTP 29, and the 2026-06-03 publish date. source-3 (reference docs) confirms the set-operations and dynamic() quotes. source-4 (January roadmap) confirms the milestone, typed-structs and type-signatures next steps, the 'v1.21 (Nov/2026) and v1.22 (May/2027)' mapping, and the '15 months' note.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote is verbatim; every version, date, function name, and OTP requirement traces to a primary source. Technical specifics are correctly cited to the primary publications (GitHub tag for date/headings, changelog for functions, reference docs for type-system definitions) per Rule 9 — no press-release-only attribution. The RC.4 internal cross-reference was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; clearly separates shipped features from the roadmap's open questions.
editor_notes.originality: Legitimate force-follow-up: the prior 2026-05 article covered RC.4 anticipating a May final; this covers the actual June 3 GA and cross-references it. No duplicate.
recommendations: elixir-lang.org and hexdocs.pm were added to the allowlist in a prior batch; the hexdocs.pm → elixir.hexdocs.pm redirect is an info-only note and non-blocking.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified follow-up with disciplined primary-source citation. APPROVE.
